sourceMovie.title: Treasure Planet
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Treasure Planet (2002) as a science fiction theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The science fiction and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Atlantis: The Lost Empire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Treasure Planet clicked for you, Atlantis: The Lost Empire (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Home
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Treasure Planet clicked for you, Home (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Treasure Planet?
faq.answer: Try Atlantis: The Lost Empire, Home, Atlantis: Milo's Return—each shares DNA with Treasure Planet while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Treasure Planet?
faq.answer: Treasure Planet (2002) is best described as Science Fiction, Adventure, Animation, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Treasure Planet worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
